phases.summary: The opening quarter is defined by set pieces, long throws and aerial contests rather than open-play rhythm. Canada use Millar’s long throw and quick restarts to pin Bosnia back, while Bosnia answer with repeated free-kicks and deliveries into the box. The commentary suggests a frantic, scrappy start with both sides competing hard for second balls and neither yet settling into a clear in-possession structure.

Canada sustained pressure in Bosnia's half after the goal
adjustments: Canada became more fluid and pushed numbers forward after the opening 15 minutes
Jonathan David began dropping into central attacking involvement while Millar and Buchanan supplied width
Bosnia leaned on dead-ball threat and a near-post corner routine to score

phases.summary: Canada started slowly but grew into the phase, moving the ball with more fluency and creating several chances through Koné, Millar, Buchanan and Jonathan David. Bosnia, while under pressure for long stretches, remained dangerous from set pieces and scored from a well-worked near-post corner to take the lead through Lukic. After the goal, Canada kept pinning Bosnia back, but their finishing and final-ball quality remained inconsistent.

This halftime phase contains no tactical detail beyond the scoreline, with Bosnia and Herzegovina leading Canada 1-0. The rest of the text shifts away from play to crowd attendance and the Bosnian manager’s appearance, so no formations, adjustments, or matchup information can be reliably extracted.

Bosnia threatening on set-pieces and direct transitions
adjustments: Canada’s triple substitution on 61' — Promise David, Shaffelburg and Ahmed on for Millar, Buchanan and Jonathan David — adding more energy and different attacking movement
Bosnia’s double change on 61' — Gigovic and Bazdar on
Ahmed drifting in from the right after coming on

key_matchups: Laryea vs Bosnia’s recovery defense, especially Kolasinac on the goal-line block
phases.summary: Canada controlled the phase territorially and repeatedly created dangerous moments, but Bosnia’s deep defending, goalkeeper intervention and especially Kolasinac’s outstanding goal-line block kept them level. After the substitutions, Canada added fresh attacking energy and continued to press, while Bosnia remained a threat from set pieces and occasional breaks, including Demirovic’s missed one-on-one.

adjustments: 78' substitute goal-scorer Cyle Larin comes on and scores the equaliser for Canada
Bosnia and Herzegovina have Edin Dzeko on the bench; the text does not confirm his introduction
phases.summary: The only clear tactical event in this late phase is Canada's equaliser through substitute Cyle Larin in the 78th minute, suggesting a successful attacking change off the bench. Beyond that, the text offers no detailed shape or pressing information, only confirming Canada and Bosnia and Herzegovina's late-stage personnel and that Bosnia's major striker Edin Dzeko remained a bench option.
